title: Missing Child in Calgary: Police Search for 11-Year-Old Parker in Cambrian Heights, Ask Residents to Check Yards and Sheds
excerpt: Calgary police are searching for Parker, 11, who went missing Thursday from a day home in the northwest community of Cambrian Heights. He has a medical condition and was last seen wearing noise-cancelling headphones, which means he may not hear people calling for him. Police are asking residents in and around Cambrian Heights to check back yards, unlocked sheds, and anywhere a child might shelter or hide.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Calgary Driver Caught Going 191 km/h on Stoney Trail While Watching a Video, Then Failed a Breathalyzer
excerpt: Calgary police stopped a driver going 191 km/h in a 100 km/h zone on southbound Stoney Trail near Glenmore Trail SE. The driver was watching a video on his phone at the time, and then failed a mandatory roadside alcohol test. His licence is suspended, his vehicle is impounded, and more fines are coming through a court summons.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Tornado Watch Issued for Lethbridge and Southern Alberta: Hail, Damaging Winds and a Tornado Risk Today
excerpt: Environment Canada has issued a tornado watch for southern Alberta, covering everywhere within about 130 kilometres of Lethbridge. Conditions are favourable for severe thunderstorms that could produce tornadoes later today, along with large hail, strong winds and heavy rain. This is a watch, not a warning, so there's still time to prepare. Here's what it covers and what to do if a warning follows.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Southern Alberta Fire Danger Is Climbing as the Heat Peaks: Where Fire Bans Are in Effect Right Now
excerpt: Southern Alberta is in the hottest, driest stretch of summer, and fire risk is climbing with it. Lethbridge's River Valley is under a full fire ban, Cypress County around Medicine Hat has had one of its worst fire seasons in years, and a dry-lightning threat is forecast to push toward the Alberta border this week. But the status is patchy, banned in some places, clear in others. Here's where things stand and how to check your own area.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Planet Fitness Is Opening a 16,000-Square-Foot Gym Inside the Medicine Hat Walmart
excerpt: A 16,000-square-foot Planet Fitness is under construction inside the Walmart at 2075 Strachan Road SE in Medicine Hat, with an opening planned for fall 2026. It's the chain's latest Canadian location, built around low-cost memberships and, for Black Card members, extras like massage loungers. Here's what's coming and what it means for the city.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Alberta First Nation Near Fort McMurray Sues Ottawa Over a $5 Treaty Payment Frozen Since 1899
excerpt: The Athabasca Chipewyan First Nation, near Fort McMurray, has filed a lawsuit against the federal government over a treaty payment that has stayed at $5 a year since Treaty 8 was signed in 1899. Chief Allan Adam says a promise paid at 1899 values is no longer a meaningful promise. The nation isn't seeking damages for past underpayment, only for the payment to be modernized. In 2023, a similar dispute in Ontario ended in a $10-billion settlement.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Coal Mining Company Fined $360,000 Over Worker's Death at Genesee Mine in Leduc County Alberta
excerpt: Prairie Mines & Royalty ULC has been penalized $360,000 after an experienced worker was killed at the Genesee coal mine near Warburg in Leduc County in November 2023. The worker was clearing ice from a water line when an ice plug blew out of the pipe and struck him. The company pleaded guilty to one charge under Alberta's OHS Act, and the money will fund research and training on the exact task that killed him.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Telus World of Science Edmonton Will Stay Closed Until November After Flood, Lays Off 147 Staff
excerpt: Edmonton's Telus World of Science says it may not reopen until around November after last Friday's storm flooded its main floor, and it has temporarily laid off 147 employees, nearly 70 per cent of its workforce. Parts of the building are contaminated badly enough that flooring, drywall and cabinetry will have to be torn out and replaced. The centre says it hopes for a partial reopening earlier in the fall.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Separatist Leader Jeffrey Rath Has $8.5 Million in Assets Frozen After First Nation Alleges He Took Trust Money
excerpt: An Alberta judge has frozen up to $8.5 million in assets belonging to lawyer Jeffrey Rath and his firm, after the Tallcree First Nation alleged in court that he moved trust money he had been ordered to repay into his own corporation. The trust holds settlement money meant for Tallcree members, including funds held for children until they turn 18. Rath denies wrongdoing, and the allegations have not been proven in court. A hearing was set for July 15.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Married Couple Killed in North Edmonton Apartment Fire After Vehicle Hit a Gas Line at 132 Avenue and 82 Street
excerpt: An elderly married couple died in a north Edmonton apartment fire that began when a vehicle drove into their building and ruptured a gas line on Monday night. An 85-year-old man died after jumping from his balcony to escape the flames, and his 82-year-old wife was also confirmed dead. Six people and the driver were taken to hospital with non-life-threatening injuries, and 35 residents were displaced.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Calgary Community Holds Candlelight Vigil for Drihanna Matamona, the 7-Year-Old Girl Who Drowned in a Legacy Pond
excerpt: Dozens gathered at a pond in Calgary's Legacy community on Tuesday night for a candlelight vigil honouring Drihanna Matamona, the seven-year-old girl who drowned there on Sunday. Her family, who organized the vigil, remembered her as a bright, joyful and deeply loved child. A second vigil is planned for Sunday, and the family has set up a fundraiser to help with expenses.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Lethbridge Street Machine Weekend: Police Issue 257 Tickets and Say a Fire Truck Got Stuck in the Crowd
excerpt: Lethbridge police issued 257 tickets over this year's Street Machine Weekend, but the bigger concern in their account is public safety. An unofficial gathering drew an estimated 2,000 people and 800 vehicles, a fire truck responding to reports of smoke got stuck in traffic gridlock, and a pedestrian was struck by a vehicle Friday night. Police say they'll review the event to decide what more is needed.
author: Adam Harrison

title: ASIRT Is Investigating After a 22-Year-Old Man Fell From Lethbridge's High Level Bridge and Died During a Police Response
excerpt: Alberta's police watchdog, ASIRT, has been called to investigate the death of a 22-year-old man who fell from Lethbridge's High Level Bridge on Sunday. Lethbridge police say officers had responded to a report of a man on the bridge deck, and that he backed away and fell as they approached. He was pronounced dead at the scene. ASIRT investigates any death that happens in connection with police, which is standard procedure.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Is Using AI to Rebuild $2 Billion Worth of Government Software, and Quebec Just Signed On to Copy It
excerpt: Alberta and Quebec signed a five-year agreement on July 14 to share artificial intelligence tools, training and code between their governments. There's no money attached; it's a knowledge-sharing pact. But behind it is a much bigger Alberta project: rebuilding the province's aging government software with AI, work Alberta says would otherwise cost $2 billion and take a century, at a claimed 95 per cent less cost.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Mount Royal University Confirms Ransomware Attack Stole Student and Staff Data. Only Employees Are Being Offered Credit Monitoring.
excerpt: Mount Royal University has confirmed that a ransomware group stole and then deleted student and employee data in a June attack, with a gang called CMD Organization demanding a $1.9 million ransom after claiming to have taken more than 10 terabytes. The university is offering two years of credit monitoring to current and former employees, but has not extended that same protection to the students whose files sat on the same compromised drive.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Banff Legacy Inn: 131 Vacation Rentals Are Going Up Beside a Hamlet of 160 People at the Park Gates
excerpt: The Banff Legacy Inn will bring 131 vacation rentals to Harvie Heights, a hamlet of about 160 people a kilometre from the Banff park gates. It was marketed to open this spring; that's now slipped to 2027. Here's what's being built, why residents fought it, and why the same fight is playing out all along the edge of the Rockies.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Red Deer Tornado Watch: Storms Move South Into Central Alberta Tonight With Large Hail and Damaging Winds
excerpt: Environment Canada has shifted its tornado watch south, and it now covers Red Deer, Lacombe and much of central Alberta. Storms developing this evening could produce a tornado, along with large hail, strong winds and heavy rain, as they move east. Environment Canada warns significant damage is possible. This is a watch, not a warning, which means there is still time to prepare.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Eight People Guilty in Alberta Cougar and Lynx Poaching Case That Started With One Out-of-Season Kill
excerpt: Eight people have pleaded guilty to 13 offences under Alberta's Wildlife Act after a two-year investigation into illegal cougar and Canada lynx hunts across the province. It started with one out-of-season cougar killed on a guided hunt near Drayton Valley and grew into 11 illegal guided hunts, $135,500 in fines, and 18.5 years of hunting bans. Officers seized 11 cougar skins and skulls and five lynx skins.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Tornado Watch for Edmonton, Spruce Grove, Stony Plain and Leduc Tonight: Large Hail, Heavy Rain and Damaging Winds Expected
excerpt: Environment Canada issued a tornado watch Tuesday afternoon covering a large area of central Alberta, from Yellowhead County to Edmonton and south to Lacombe. Storms developing this evening have the potential to produce a tornado, along with large hail, strong winds and heavy rain. The storms will track east through the evening. This is a watch, not a warning, which means there is still time to prepare.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Grande Prairie Crane Operator's Body Recovered From the Wapiti River, Four Weeks After the Highway 40 Bridge Collapse
excerpt: Clairmont RCMP confirmed on July 14 that they have recovered the remains of the crane operator who died when his crane went through the Wapiti River Bridge railing on June 16. He had been in the water for four weeks. The crane itself is still being pulled from the river, and RCMP are asking the public to keep boats and drones out of the area.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Calgary Stampede 2026 Attendance: 1,411,954 Guests, the Third-Highest Ever, but Down From Two Straight Record Years
excerpt: The Calgary Stampede released its final attendance figures Monday: 1,411,954 guests over ten days, with 122,559 on the closing Sunday. It's the third-best turnout in the event's history, but it comes after back-to-back records in 2024 and 2025, and in a year with exceptionally good weather. CEO Joel Cowley used his closing press conference to argue that attendance isn't the most important measure of success.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Calgary Plumbing Company Fined $330,000 Three Years After a Trench Collapse Killed 27-Year-Old Liam Johnston
excerpt: Mr. Mike's Plumbing Ltd. pleaded guilty on July 13 to one charge under Alberta's OHS Act, three years after apprentice plumber Liam Johnston was buried in an unshored trench in northwest Calgary. The company was fined $115,000 and ordered to pay a further $215,000 to fund a provincial campaign teaching young workers to speak up about unsafe work. Court heard Johnston had raised concerns about the site the day before he died.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Calgary Massage Therapist Charged With Sexual Assault, in a Profession Alberta Still Doesn't Regulate
excerpt: Yuriy Zhivov, 52, has been charged with one count of sexual assault after a client reported being touched sexually during an August 2025 appointment at a Calgary clinic. Police are asking anyone with information about this "or similar incidents" to come forward. It's at least the fourth such charge against a Calgary massage therapist in under two years, and Alberta remains one of the only provinces where massage therapy is not a regulated health profession.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Edmonton Storm Damage: Telus World of Science and Two Pools Still Closed, Fire Crews Hit Record Call Volume
excerpt: Severe weather over the weekend flooded roads, downed trees and traffic poles, and put water inside several City of Edmonton facilities. Edmonton Fire Rescue Services responded to a record-high volume of calls. Telus World of Science and two pools remain closed, and the city is extending Eco Station hours for two weeks so residents can dump storm debris. Here's
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta, Ottawa and Five Oilsands Producers Sign the Pathways Deal: No West Coast Pipeline Without Carbon Capture
excerpt: Alberta, the federal government and the five largest oilsands producers have signed a memorandum of understanding to build Pathways, a carbon capture network anchored by a storage hub near Cold Lake. It's the condition Ottawa set for a new West Coast oil pipeline. Alberta wants to double production to eight million barrels a day. Here's what was actually agreed, and what still hasn't been.
author: Adam Harrison

title: A 6-Year-Old Girl Has Drowned in a Calgary Pond. It's the Latest in a Devastating Run of Water Deaths in the City's Southeast
excerpt: A six-year-old girl has died after drowning in a pond in the southeast Calgary community of Legacy on Sunday afternoon. A family member pulled her from the water before crews arrived, and firefighters performed CPR, but she was declared deceased at the scene. Her death follows three drownings at nearby Mahogany Lake in less than a year.
author: Adam Harrison

title: E-Scooter Injuries Are Surging Across Alberta, and Edmonton Now Leads the Province With 1,198 ER Visits in One Year
excerpt: Alberta Health Services data shows 3,049 people visited emergency rooms and urgent care centres across Alberta for e-scooter injuries in the most recent reporting year, up 33 per cent. Edmonton accounted for 1,198 of them, up from 813 the year before, and the number of kids treated at the Stollery more than doubled. Doctors say the injuries are getting worse, not just more common.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Calgary Landlord Charged With Voyeurism After Hidden Cameras Found in Tenants' Bathrooms and Bedrooms
excerpt: Calgary police have charged a 41-year-old landlord with voyeurism and break and enter after tenants found hidden cameras disguised as a surge protector and a smoke detector in their bathroom and bedrooms. Police believe Sooryong Park, who also uses the name Nick Park, may have operated as a landlord in Vancouver and Toronto, targeting women in the Korean community. Anyone with information is asked to contact police.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Amber Alert Cancelled, but 6-Year-Old Lanakai Morrison Is Still Missing: RCMP Say the Public Can No Longer Help
excerpt: Beaverlodge RCMP cancelled the Amber Alert for six-year-old Lanakai Morrison on the afternoon of July 10, but the boy is still missing, along with his mother, her partner, and a four-year-old child. Police say the alert was pulled because there is no reasonable expectation the public in Alberta can act on it. The last confirmed sighting was in Fort St. John, B.C. Anyone with information is still asked to call 911.
author: Adam Harrison

title: 7 great things to do in Calgary this weekend: July 10 to 12
excerpt: Stampede rides into Championship Sunday, the Wild host Vancouver at McMahon, and the zoo is doing breakfast with the polar bears. Here are 7 ways to close out Calgary's biggest week.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Severe Storm Hits Edmonton, St. Albert, Spruce Grove, and Stony Plain Tonight: Large Hail and Damaging Winds
excerpt: An intense line of severe thunderstorms is moving across the Edmonton region the evening of July 10, with the heaviest core tracking over St. Albert and pushing toward Edmonton. Spruce Grove, Stony Plain, and Morinville are in the path too. Environment Canada warns of large hail, damaging winds, and heavy rain, with an isolated tornado risk. Warnings are firing cell by cell, so check the alert for your own community.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Edmonton Police Ask for Help After a Teen Was Sexually Assaulted by a Stranger in the River Valley
excerpt: Edmonton police are asking the public for help identifying a man who sexually assaulted a teenage girl in the river valley west of Grand View Drive on the evening of June 23. The girl broke away and ran to a nearby neighbourhood. Investigators have released a description of the suspect and want to hear from anyone who was in the area.
author: Adam Harrison

title: 12 great things to do in Edmonton this weekend: July 10 to 12
excerpt: Four festivals play their final weekend, Whyte Avenue turns into an open-air gallery, and Saturday starts with free pancakes in Old Strathcona. Here's your Edmonton weekend, July 10 to 12.
author: Culture Alberta

title: Online Gambling Becomes Legal in Alberta This Monday, July 13: The Rules, the Brands, and What It Means for Albertans
excerpt: Alberta opens its regulated online gambling market this Monday, July 13, becoming the second province after Ontario to let private betting and casino companies operate legally. Big brands like FanDuel, DraftKings, and BetMGM are lined up, the province takes 20 percent of their revenue, the legal age is 18, and a new tool lets people block themselves from every licensed site at once. Here's what actually changes.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Grande Prairie Driver Clocked at 202 km/h on Highway 43 Near Wembley, Nearly Double the Limit
excerpt: Clairmont RCMP say a 46-year-old Grande Prairie driver was caught going 202 km/h in a 110 zone on Highway 43 between Grande Prairie and Wembley late on July 6. The driver was hit with a mandatory court summons for speeding and careless driving. At that speed, a car covers the length of a football field in under two seconds.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Tornado Warning Near Edmonton: Alberta Emergency Alert Issued for Barrhead County and Thunder Lake Tonight
excerpt: Environment Canada issued a tornado warning the evening of July 9 for the County of Barrhead near Thunder Lake and Holmes Crossing, about 120 km northwest of Edmonton. Meteorologists are tracking a severe thunderstorm that may be producing a tornado, with damaging winds, large hail, and heavy rain also possible. Officials call it a dangerous, potentially life-threatening situation. Take cover immediately if the storm approaches.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Edmonton Breaks Ground on 68 Affordable Homes in Mill Woods, Part of a Plan to Turn 11 Old School Sites Into 1,300 Homes
excerpt: Right at Home Housing Society broke ground July 9 on a $23.6-million affordable housing development in Kiniski Gardens, bringing 68 townhomes to Mill Woods. It's one of 11 surplus school sites Edmonton is converting into more than 1,300 new homes, roughly 950 of them affordable, backed by nearly $100 million in city money and the federal Housing Accelerator Fund. Here's what's being built and how the plan works.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Amber Alert Still Active for 6-Year-Old Boy Taken Near Grande Prairie: RCMP Now Searching for a Red Ford F350
excerpt: The Amber Alert for six-year-old Lanakai Morrison, abducted near Grande Prairie on July 7, remains active into Thursday evening. RCMP have updated key details: the group is now believed to be in a 2006 red Ford F350, Alberta plate CXW8820, after officers found the black Toyota Tundra from the original alert. Police say the case stems from a family court order and are urging anyone who sees them to call 911, not approach.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Medicine Hat Approves Affordable Seniors Housing for Indigenous Elders on Spencer Street: What's Being Built and When
excerpt: Medicine Hat city council has unanimously approved donating a surplus parcel at 301 Spencer Street SE to the Miywasin Friendship Centre, along with a $412,000 transfer from capital reserves, to build affordable housing for Indigenous Elders. The project will bring 16 to 20 independent-living units to a piece of city land that has sat empty since the 1970s. Here is what's planned, how it's funded, and the timeline.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Tornado Tears Through Dillberry Lake Campground Near Lloydminster, Campers Sent to Hospital
excerpt: A confirmed tornado touched down at Dillberry Lake Provincial Park on the evening of July 8, flipping a trailer, damaging structures, and sending campers to hospital. Everyone at the campground has been accounted for, RCMP say, and neighbours turned out with saws and tractors to clear the roads. It hit the exact area we flagged under an emergency alert hours earlier.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Amber Alert in Effect for 6-Year-Old Lanakai Morrison, Abducted Near Beaverlodge, Alberta
excerpt: Alberta RCMP issued an Amber Alert late July 8 for six-year-old Lanakai Morrison, who police say was abducted near Beaverlodge on July 7 by his mother. He may be in a black 2015 Toyota Tundra with Alberta plate CTN9517. The alert covers everyone within 200 km of Beaverlodge, including Grande Prairie. Do not approach. Call Beaverlodge RCMP at 780-354-2955 with any information.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Calgary Police Shooting Victim Identified as Melanie Hambrook, 53: Family Says She Was in a Mental-Health Crisis
excerpt: The family of the woman shot and killed by Calgary police on Deerfoot Trail on June 21 has named her as Melanie Hambrook, a 53-year-old insurance broker, painter, and mother. They say she was in a mental-health crisis, not a criminal one, and they want to know why she died. ASIRT, which says she was armed with a knife, is still investigating.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Emergency Alert Near Lloydminster: Tornado Warning and Funnel Cloud Reported at Paradise Valley Tonight
excerpt: Environment Canada issued a tornado warning the evening of July 8 for the County of Vermilion River near Paradise Valley, after a funnel cloud was reported. The severe storm line is tracking east toward Lloydminster with damaging winds, ping-pong-ball hail, and flooding rain. Officials call it a dangerous, potentially life-threatening situation. If the storm reaches you, take cover immediately.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Meta Is Building a $13 Billion AI Data Centre in Alberta, Its First in Canada, in Sturgeon County North of Edmonton
excerpt: Meta announced July 8 it will build a massive AI data centre in Sturgeon County, a C$13 billion investment and the company's first in Canada. The 1-gigawatt facility will run on its own dedicated natural gas power plant and use roughly as much electricity as 800,000 homes. It confirms the identity of the mystery customer behind a $4.6 billion gas plant announced in the same area last week.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Lethbridge Drug House Shut Down for 90 Days After Neighbours' Complaints
excerpt: A north Lethbridge home tied to persistent drug activity has been ordered closed for 90 days after a SCAN investigation that started in March 2025. The property was boarded up and fenced off at noon on July 7 and stays closed until October 5, with conditions in place for two more years. Police seized drugs at the home last year and arrested four people.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Storm Watch: Damaging Hail, 100 km/h Winds and a Tornado Risk Sweep the Province Today
excerpt: Environment Canada has much of Alberta under a severe thunderstorm watch for the afternoon and evening of July 8, with storms capable of very large hail, wind gusts past 100 kilometres an hour, and heavy rain. A tornado can't be ruled out. The threat is shifting through the day, so the smart move is to check the live alert for your own town before you head out.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Son of Former Edmonton Councillor Among 28 Arrested Worldwide in Dark Web Child Sexual Exploitation Investigation
excerpt: ALERT says its Internet Child Exploitation unit arrested a 40-year-old Stony Plain man on May 27 as part of Operation Torch, a Norway-led investigation that led to 28 arrests across 14 countries. Investigators allege the suspects used cryptocurrency to buy access to dark web forums hosting child sexual abuse material. The man is the son of former Edmonton councillor Tony Caterina, ALERT says. The allegations have not been tested in court.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Brian Paisley, the Man Who Started the Edmonton Fringe and Sparked Fringe Festivals Across North America, Has Died at 79
excerpt: Brian Paisley launched the Edmonton International Fringe Festival in 1982 with what he called an absurd idea, that anyone could be an artist and tell a story without gatekeepers. It became the first Fringe in North America and the largest outside Edinburgh. He died in Mexico on July 2 at 79, a month before the festival's next season. Edmonton's theatre community called him Father Fringe.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Grande Prairie Crane Recovery Begins on Highway 40, Three Weeks After the Fatal Wapiti River Bridge Crash
excerpt: Crews have started pulling the crane out of the Wapiti River south of Grande Prairie, three weeks after it went through the Highway 40 bridge railing and killed the operator inside. RCMP are asking boats to stay off the river and warning drivers to expect delays. The operator has still not been recovered, and the cause of the crash remains unknown.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Sportsnet 960 and 660 News Are Gone. Calgary Loses Both Its Sports and News Radio Stations as Rogers Cuts 230 Jobs
excerpt: Rogers Sports and Media closed six radio stations across four Canadian cities on July 7, including both of Calgary's flagship stations: Sportsnet 960 The Fan and 660 NewsRadio. The cuts affect 230 jobs company-wide, 80 of them in radio. Rogers will no longer produce Calgary Flames radio broadcasts. The closures came one day after Rogers paid $4.35 billion to become sole owner of Maple Leaf Sports and Entertainment.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Lethbridge Firefighters Rescued Six People from the Oldman River Weir. Their Raft Had Deflated. None Were Wearing Life Jackets.
excerpt: Lethbridge Fire and Emergency Services responded to the Oldman River weir on the evening of July 5 after six boaters were caught in dangerous high-water conditions when their inflatable raft deflated. None of the six were wearing personal flotation devices. Crews from four stations, eight emergency apparatus, and 18 firefighters responded. The Water Rescue Team deployed two boats and brought all six safely to shore. No injuries were reported. A boating advisory remains in effect for the Oldman River.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Alberta Is Putting $15 Million Into Highway 63 After Fort McMurray Residents Filled the Potholes Themselves
excerpt: The Alberta government announced $22.4 million in additional highway maintenance funding on July 5, with $15 million directed specifically to Highway 63. The announcement follows months of complaints from Fort McMurray residents about potholes damaging vehicles, a June 12 volunteer pothole-filling operation, and pressure from local MLAs, the RMWB mayor, and the Conservative MP for the region. New repaving contracts are being expedited for 48 kilometres near Wandering River and 30 kilometres in the Fort McMurray area.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Smith and Ford Just Announced the Northern Shield Energy Corridor, a 3,300-Kilometre Oil Pipeline From Alberta to Ontario
excerpt: Premiers Danielle Smith and Doug Ford unveiled the proposed route for the Northern Shield Energy Corridor in Calgary on Monday. The pipeline would run 3,300 kilometres from Hardisty, Alberta through Regina and Winnipeg to Sarnia, Ontario. Initial capacity is 500,000 barrels per day with expansion possible to 800,000. A feasibility study is underway and due by end of 2026. No cost has been confirmed and no construction has been approved yet.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Southern Alberta Is Under an Orange Severe Thunderstorm Alert Right Now. Damaging Hail, 100 km/h Winds, and a Tornado Risk This Afternoon
excerpt: Environment and Climate Change Canada issued an orange alert Sunday morning for potentially dangerous thunderstorm activity across a large section of southern Alberta including Calgary, Lethbridge, Medicine Hat, Brooks, Taber, and Claresholm. Conditions are favourable for dangerous thunderstorms this afternoon and into this evening. Most intense activity is expected around 4 p.m.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Severe Thunderstorms Are Targeting Alberta Today. Six-Centimetre Hail and Tornado Risk for Parts of the Province
excerpt: Environment Canada's Storm Prediction Centre has flagged large portions of Alberta at risk of severe thunderstorms on July 5. Southern Alberta faces the highest risk with hail up to six centimetres, wind gusts up to 110 km/h, and a tornado risk. Calgary, Edmonton, Medicine Hat, and Lethbridge face a moderate risk with four-centimetre hail possible. A severe thunderstorm warning was already issued Saturday for parts of central Alberta including Lacombe and Ponoka counties.
author: Adam Harrison

title: RCMP Fatally Shot a Man on Highway 9 Northeast of Calgary. ASIRT Is Investigating
excerpt: Airdrie RCMP responded to an open-line 911 call on Highway 9 just outside Irricana at approximately 6:20 p.m. on July 3. Officers located a male suspect and an altercation occurred. At least one officer discharged their service weapon. The man was pronounced dead at the scene despite life-saving measures. No officers were injured. ASIRT is now investigating.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Sikome Lake Is Back for Summer 2026. Calgary's Favourite Beach Just Got a $6 Million Upgrade.
excerpt: The Sikome Aquatic Facility at Fish Creek Provincial Park reopened for the 2026 season on June 29 following $6 million in upgrades spread over four years. Two new admissions booths, a new water safety centre, renovations to five existing buildings, and surface restoration are all complete. Sikome draws about 100,000 visitors each season and has been serving Calgarians for nearly 50 years.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Free Calgary Stampede Pancake Breakfasts 2026: Full List by Day, Time, and Location
excerpt: Free pancake breakfasts happen across Calgary every day of the 2026 Calgary Stampede, July 3 to 12, with several starting July 2. Green tea pancakes on Macleod Trail. A competition between 13 Mission restaurants for a Golden Spatula. A Filipino breakfast with garlic fried rice and longganisa. A dog-friendly breakfast. And Fluor Rope Square running free every single morning downtown. Here is the full guide organized by day.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Lloydminster's Costco Just Confirmed a November Opening. Location, Jobs, Gas Bar and What to Expect
excerpt: A Costco manager confirmed at a pop-up membership event in Lloydminster that the new warehouse is on track to open in the second or third week of November 2026. The store is under construction at 75 Avenue and 19 Street. It will be approximately 160,000 square feet with 900 parking stalls. Local hiring of 100 to 150 people begins around September. The gas bar will offer diesel, gas, and propane. Lloydminster has been waiting for this since 2019.
author: Adam Harrison

title: ASIRT Clears Lethbridge Police After Woman Dies in Custody in 2023. The Report Details Missed Checks and a Fatal Overdose
excerpt: ASIRT has released its findings into the September 2, 2023 death of a woman in Lethbridge Police Service custody. No criminal charges will be laid against any officer or staff member. The report documents missed wellness checks, a supervising officer who nudged an unresponsive woman with his foot and left, and a commissionaire who recorded checks she did not complete. The woman died of acute p-fluorofentanyl intoxication. The investigation took nearly three years.
author: Adam Harrison

title: More Than 106,000 Albertans Have Applied for the $100 Energy Rebate. Here Is What to Do If the Portal Did Not Work for You
excerpt: More than 106,000 Albertans applied for the $100 Alberta Energy Rebate within the first 24 hours of the portal opening July 1. Two problems hit on launch day a sign-in issue that has since been fixed, and a bank verification problem that is still catching people. ATB Financial is not on the supported bank list. Here is what went wrong and exactly what to do if the portal did not work for you.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Grande Prairie Fire Advisory Lifted June 30. Fire Pits, Campfires and Burn Barrels Are Permitted Again.
excerpt: Effective 9 a.m. on June 30, 2026, the County of Grande Prairie Regional Fire Service lifted the fire advisory for the entire county and the towns of Beaverlodge, Sexsmith, and Wembley. The City of Grande Prairie lifted its advisory the same morning. Alberta Wildfire also lifted all restrictions in the Grande Prairie Forest Area the same day. Here is exactly what you can and cannot do now that the advisory is gone.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Edmonton traffic stop leads to arrest of man tied to extortion network and murder conspiracy warrants
excerpt: A May 4 traffic stop on a Dodge Charger in Edmonton led police to Safaldeep Singh, 22, who has suspected ties to the "For Brothers" gang a criminal network linked to shootings and extortions of South Asian business owners across Canada and the United States. Singh was arrested June 23 on local charges and transferred to Ontario on Canada-wide warrants for conspiracy to commit murder and extortion. The investigation is part of Project Insight, EPS's framework for tackling South Asian extortion networks.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta's New Driver's Licence With Your Health Card Number and Citizenship Status Is Now Live
excerpt: The Government of Alberta launched its redesigned driver's licence and ID card on July 2, 2026. The new card includes your personal health number and a Canadian citizenship marker. No more separate paper health card. No additional cost. You get the new card when you next renew. Alberta's privacy commissioner published guidance today on how to protect yourself. Here is everything you need to know.
author: Adam Harrison

title: You Can Now Drink Beer at a Picnic Table in Medicine Hat. Here Is Everything You Need to Know
excerpt: Starting July 2, 2026, Medicine Hat residents can consume alcohol at designated picnic tables at fireplaces in three city parks: Strathcona Island Park, Kin Coulee Park, and Echo Dale Regional Park. The pilot runs until September 30. All spots are first come, first served. A resident survey launches at medicinehat.ca. Council will decide whether to make it permanent once the pilot ends.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Medicine Hat Natural Gas Rate Dropped in July After June’s 76 Percent Spike. Here Is the Full Breakdown
excerpt: Medicine Hat's natural gas rate spiked 76 percent in June. In July it is coming back down — but not all the way. The City set July's rate at $1.362 per gigajoule, down from June's $1.721 but still 39 percent above May's historic low of $0.979. Here is the full 2026 rate history, what it means on your bill, what is happening with faulty meters, and how Medicine Hat's rates compare to the rest of Alberta.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Medicine Hat Has Launched an Independent Investigation Into the Death of an 11-Year-Old Child at Echo Dale Regional Park
excerpt: The City of Medicine Hat announced June 30 that the Lifesaving Society of Alberta and NWT Branch will begin an independent review of the incident at Echo Dale Regional Park that resulted in the death of an 11-year-old child on June 15. The review begins July 2 and includes a documentation review, a site inspection, and interviews with staff. The final report could take up to 60 days. The swim lake at Echo Dale remains closed.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Edmonton Police Are Warning Alberta Businesses About Two Fraud Schemes That Have Already Cost Companies More Than $300,000
excerpt: The Edmonton Police Service Financial Crimes Section is warning Canadian businesses about two sophisticated fraud schemes — impersonation fraud and chargeback fraud — that have resulted in more than $300,000 in combined losses across Canada. In one case alone, a local Edmonton business lost $50,000 in product. Officers intercepted $130,000 worth of goods before they could be shipped overseas. Most of the product ends up in shipping containers bound for ports in Africa.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Lethbridge Urges Residents to Stay Away from the Oldman River on Canada Day as Flows Hit 900 Cubic Metres Per Second
excerpt: The City of Lethbridge issued an urgent update at 9:45 a.m. on July 1 warning residents to stay away from the Oldman River after significant rainfall caused flows to rise to 900 cubic metres per second. That is higher than the peaks seen during the June flooding events and well above the normal Canada Day flow of 200 to 250 cubic metres per second. Pavan Park, Popson Park, Alexander Wilderness Park, and Cottonwood Park are all closed. No flood risk to homes or infrastructure has been identified
author: Shannon Williams

title: Lethbridge Closed Its Last Harm Reduction Site Today After Overdose Deaths Fell 89 Percent. Not Everyone Thinks That's Why
excerpt: The Lethbridge Overdose Prevention Site closed July 1, 2026 after six years of operation outside the Lethbridge Wellness Shelter. The City of Lethbridge requested the closure in a January 2026 letter to the province following a 7-2 council vote in December 2025. In its place: a RAAM clinic, 10 new withdrawal beds, and a 24-hour outreach team. Overdose deaths in Lethbridge are down 89.7 percent since 2023 according to city statistics cited by Mayor Hyggen. Harm reduction experts say closing the site is not evidence-based
author: Adam Harrison

title: Class Action Lawsuit Launched After Alberta Voter List Breach Exposes 2.9 Million Residents
excerpt: A proposed class-action lawsuit was filed June 26 in Edmonton's Court of King's Bench alleging the personal information of approximately 2.9 million Alberta voters was unlawfully accessed and distributed. The lawsuit names the Alberta government, the chief electoral officer, the Centurion Project Ltd., the Republican Party of Alberta, and David Parker as defendants. The action must still be certified by a judge before it can proceed. None of the allegations have been proven in court
author: Shannon Williams

title: A Massive New $97 Million Amazon Delivery Station Is Coming to Northwest Edmonton
excerpt: Amazon has received a City of Edmonton building permit for a new delivery station at 16404 145 Avenue NW, with an estimated construction value of $97 million. The site sits in the Apex Business Park, on 50 acres Amazon purchased for $39.7 million in late 2025. There is no confirmed opening date yet, but construction is expected to begin this year. The project will sit just north of Twin Willows Golf Club, which is permanently closing after this season
author: Adam Harrison

title: A Former Grande Prairie Peace Officer Avoided Jail After Pleading Guilty to Possessing 254 Images and 98 Videos of Child Sexual Abuse Material
excerpt: Daniel Emond, 35, a former City of Grande Prairie peace officer, was sentenced June 29 at Edmonton Court of King's Bench to a 24-month conditional sentence served on house arrest, followed by 12 months of probation. ALERT confirmed his arrest in September 2023 after tracing an uploaded image to his device. CBC News reported investigators found 254 images and 98 videos of child sexual abuse material on a device in his home.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Albertans Can Apply for the $100 Energy Rebate Starting This Week. Here Is Everything That Has Changed Since the Announcement.
excerpt: Albertans can start applying for the $100 Alberta Energy Rebate starting July 1, with the secure online portal launching this week. Nearly 3.4 million residents are eligible. The province has now confirmed the rebate formally replaces the Fuel Tax Relief Program that would have taken effect this quarter, delivering 50 percent more savings than fuel tax relief would have provided. Here is what is confirmed now that the portal is about to go live, and what is still unknown.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Stephen Avenue $36M Revitalization Completed Just in Time for Calgary Stampede
excerpt: Phase 1 of Calgary's Stephen Avenue Revitalization is substantially complete, with the block between 1 Street SE and Centre Street ready ahead of the 2026 Stampede. The $36.2 million project replaced century-old water, power, and stormwater infrastructure beneath the street and added granite pavers, new lighting, and patio power outlets above it. Future phases west of Centre Street remain unfunded
author: Adam Harrison

title: Canada Day 2026 Alberta: What's Open, What's Closed, and Where to Go
excerpt: Canada Day 2026 falls on a Wednesday, so there is no automatic long weekend this year. Banks, Canada Post, and most government offices are closed across Alberta. Costco is closed. Grocery and liquor stores stay open with varying hours by location. Here is what is open, what is closed, where to go, what you are owed if you work the holiday, and whether the festivities continue into the weekend.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Edmonton Youth Volleyball Coach Pleads Guilty to Child Sexual Abuse Material. Police Fear There May Be More Victims
excerpt: Steven Cabral, 33, pleaded guilty on June 15 to possessing child sexual abuse material — nearly 13 months after his arrest. Edmonton Police say new information uncovered during their continued investigation suggests there may be additional complainants. Cabral coached and refereed youth volleyball in Edmonton with players between the ages of 12 and 17. He is out on court-imposed conditions with a sentencing hearing scheduled for October.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Calgary Has Banned All Activity on the Bow and Elbow Rivers as Canmore and Kananaskis Declare States of Emergency
excerpt: The Calgary Fire Department issued a River Safety Advisory on June 29 ordering everyone off the Bow and Elbow Rivers immediately. Both rivers have exceeded safe flow thresholds. The advisory is expected to last through Canada Day and into Stampede week. Canmore and the Kananaskis Improvement District have both declared states of local emergency. Highway 40 is closed with multiple washouts and bridge failures. Landslides have closed Highway 93 North in Banff National Park.
author: Adam Harrison

title: An Edmonton Truck Parts Company Has Been Fined $138,000 After a Worker Was Struck by a Hood and Fell Into a Drop Pit
excerpt: Fleet Brake Parts & Service Ltd. pleaded guilty on June 24 in Edmonton Court of Justice to one count under Alberta's OHS Code for failing to protect a worker from falling. The incident happened January 22, 2024 at an Edmonton heavy-duty truck parts and service centre. A worker was struck by a vehicle hood and fell into an open drop pit, suffering serious injuries. The company was fined $138,000 including the victim fine surcharge.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Four People Are Facing Robbery Charges After Three St. Albert Stores Were Hit Within 90 Minutes on Friday Evening
excerpt: St. Albert RCMP responded to three robberies between 6:44 p.m. and 8:01 p.m. on June 26 — two liquor stores and a clothing store hit within 90 minutes. A firearm was produced in two of the incidents. A member of the public helped police catch the fourth suspect on foot. A stolen vehicle, a sword, bear spray, and break-in tools were seized. Four people from Edmonton and Wetaskiwin are now facing charges.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Edson Is Getting an $80 Million Recreation Complex With a New Pool, Arena, Gym, and Curling Facility. Here Is the Full Timeline
excerpt: The YCE Multiplex is an $80.2 million joint project between the Town of Edson and Yellowhead County currently under construction on the site of the existing Edson and District Leisure Centre. The facility will include a new pool, new ice arena, new gymnasium with elevated walking track, and a curling facility. The new aquatics area and arena are targeted for December 2026. Full completion is expected by summer 2027. The existing facility stays open throughout construction.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Lloydminster RCMP Are Looking for Four Vehicles After Shots Were Fired Near the Travelodge Friday Evening
excerpt: Lloydminster RCMP responded to a report of shots fired near the Lloydminster Travelodge at approximately 7:57 p.m. on June 26. Police are looking for two white four-door BMW SUVs, a black Dodge Ram pickup truck, and a dark-coloured Hyundai sedan. Three persons of interest have been described. RCMP say there is no ongoing threat to public safety.
author: Adam Harrison

title: A Seven-Hour Standoff on Upland Drive SE Ended with Two Medicine Hat Men in Custody and 41 Criminal Code Charges
excerpt: Medicine Hat Police responded to a high-risk incident at a residence in the 1900 block of Upland Drive SE at 2:43 p.m. on June 24. A victim was removed from the home. One man was arrested after negotiators made contact. A second man barricaded himself inside with a weapon for hours before the TACT team entered at 9:46 p.m. The second man faces 41 Criminal Code charges from seven outstanding warrants.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Carney Is Bringing His Brexit Warning to the Calgary Stampede as Alberta's Separation Vote Looms
excerpt: Prime Minister Mark Carney confirmed Thursday he is attending the Calgary Stampede next week to make the case against Alberta separation. He called the October 19 referendum a dangerous bluff, said the situation is very reminiscent of Brexit on its ten-year anniversary, and warned a yes vote would cost Canada its reputation as one of the most trustworthy countries in the world to do business with. Dozens of Liberal MPs are coming with him.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Lethbridge Has No Commercial Flights for the First Time in Decades. The Hunt for a Replacement Has Begun
excerpt: WestJet flew its last flight out of Lethbridge Regional Airport on June 24. No replacement carrier has been confirmed. The airport still handles about 100 movements a day through medevac, charter, flight training, and agricultural aviation. The city has a 2026 Airport Workplan in place and is in active talks with carriers. The province is willing to spend up to $5 million to underwrite a replacement carrier. Submissions close July 6.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Edmonton Police Fatally Shoot 59-Year-Old Armed Man During Confrontation in Southwest Edmonton. ASIRT Is Investigating.
excerpt: At 11:58 p.m. on June 26, Edmonton police responded to a collision between a vehicle and a fire rescue truck in southwest Edmonton. The driver allegedly assaulted firefighters with a blunt object and fled. Two more hit-and-run assaults followed. Officers traced the vehicle to a residence on Christie Vista SW where a confrontation occurred. The man was armed with edged weapons. One officer fired. The 59-year-old man was pronounced dead at the scene. No officers or emergency personnel were injured. ASIRT is investigating.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Champion Petfoods Opens $32 Million Pet Food Research Facility in Acheson, Parkland County, Just West of Edmonton
excerpt: Champion Petfoods opened its Global Innovation and Discovery Centre at its NorthStar Kitchen in Acheson, Parkland County on June 24. The $32 million facility was funded by parent company Mars and will be used to develop and test new recipes and technologies for its ORIJEN and ACANA brands before scaling to full production. An additional $15 million in Canadian facility investment is planned over the next five years. Champion sells its products in more than 80 markets worldwide and recently marked its 40th anniversary.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Calgary Man Charged with Manslaughter After 16-Year-Old Died of Carfentanil Overdose at His Home
excerpt: Steve Phillip Gilbert Collins, 40, was taken into custody June 25 and charged with one count of manslaughter in connection with the death of 16-year-old Jordynn Atkins-Materi on November 28, 2025. Police say the girl and a friend met Collins downtown the night before and went back to his home in the 8300 block of Centre Street NE. She was found unconscious the next morning. Calgary Police say this is the first time the service has ever laid a manslaughter charge in connection with a drug overdose death.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Edmonton Is About to Break a 112-Year Rainfall Record as Another Major Storm Brings Flooding This Weekend
excerpt: Environment Canada issued orange-level rainfall warnings for Edmonton, Sherwood Park, St. Albert, and a wide band of central Alberta on Friday morning. Between 40 and 70mm of rain is expected through the weekend with the heaviest rain Saturday. Edmonton has already recorded 203mm of rain this month. The all-time June record is 216.5mm set in 1914. The ground is saturated. Flooding is expected again.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Canada Day 2026 in Grande Prairie: Parade Route, Fireworks Location, Entertainment Lineup, and What Is Open
excerpt: Canada Day in Grande Prairie is Wednesday July 1. The parade starts at 2 p.m. downtown. Muskoseepi Park activities run from 3:30 p.m. to 10:30 p.m. Fireworks are at a new location this year — Legion Field at 11313 105 Street — at 11:30 p.m. Oliver Dawson is the 2026 Parade Marshal. Everything is free.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Viral Video Shows a Grizzly Bear Charging a Woman and Her Dog on an Alberta Trail While Hiking
excerpt: A woman walking her dog near Mount Engadine Lodge in Kananaskis Country on June 24 came within a few feet of a grizzly bear that charged her multiple times before retreating. The video has been seen by millions. She walked away unharmed. She did not have bear spray. Alberta Parks has issued a bear warning for the area.
author: Adam Harrison

title: A Grande Prairie Teen Raised $44,000 and Organized the City's First Youth Festival While Finishing High School
excerpt: EJ Igao, a graduating student at Composite High School, raised more than $44,000 in sponsorships and organized the first-ever GP Youth Fest at Muskoseepi Park on June 25. Talent show, basketball tournament, live music, food trucks, and guest speakers — all built by a teenager who was tired of hearing people call Grande Prairie boring.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Grande Prairie's $16.9 Million Multisport Dome Is Delayed. It Now Opens in Early 2027 Instead of This Summer
excerpt: The City of Grande Prairie announced June 25 that its multisport dome at Trader Ridge will not be ready for public use until early 2027. The original completion target was July 2026. The city cited remaining coordination, servicing, grading, and approval requirements as the reason for the delay. No specific cause was given. The project remains active and construction continues.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Grande Prairie Man Charged with 17 Sex Crimes After Being Released from Custody Three Times in Two Months
excerpt: Joseph Kidd, 32, of Grande Prairie faces 17 charges from four RCMP investigations between April 17 and June 18. Alleged victims range in age from 12 to 18. Kidd was released from custody three times during the investigation period and allegedly offended again each time. Some offences are alleged to have occurred while he was under court-imposed conditions. He is in custody with a court date of July 2
author: Adam Harrison

title: Multi-Vehicle Crash Closes Highway 63 Bridge to Nowhere North of Fort McMurray
excerpt: Northbound traffic on the Highway 63 bridge north of Fort McMurray came to a halt Thursday morning following a multi-vehicle collision. Wood Buffalo RCMP say the road remains impassable. Injury details have not been released. Drivers are asked to avoid the area and expect significant delays.
author: Adam Harrison

title: West Lethbridge Is Getting a $300 Million Development. A Hotel, Wendy's, 450 Homes, and a Seniors Facility Are All Coming to The Crossings
excerpt: The Core Crossing development broke ground May 29, 2026 in west Lethbridge with a total estimated investment of $300 million. The project brings the first full-service hotel in West Lethbridge, a new Wendy's, 100,000 square feet of mixed-use commercial space, 450 residential units, and a new Green Acres Foundation seniors facility. Completion is expected by 2030.
author: Adam Harrison

title: WestJet Just Flew Out of Medicine Hat for the Last Time. The City Has Contacted 22 Airlines and Found No Replacement Yet
excerpt: WestJet's Q400 aircraft taxied out of Medicine Hat Regional Airport for the last time just before 10 a.m. on June 25. No replacement carrier has been found. The airport faces a near $500,000 annual deficit. The province's Request for Expressions of Interest closes July 6. The city has contacted 22 airlines. Medicine Hat is now the largest city in Alberta without scheduled commercial air service.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Three Medicine Hat Residents Charged After Armed Home Invasion on Ross Street. All Three Are in Custody
excerpt: Three people forced their way into a home on Ross Street SE in Medicine Hat just before 1 a.m. on June 23, confronted the occupants with weapons, and were taken into custody when police arrived. Trevor Muise, 46, of Redcliff, and Keera Whiteford-Wall, 40, and Corey Straub, 55, both of Medicine Hat, face charges including break and enter to commit robbery, disguise with intent, uttering threats, and unlawful confinement. All three were remanded into custody and appeared in court June 24.
author: Adam Harrison

title: McDonald’s to Open First Indigenous-Designed Restaurant Near Calgary on Tsuut’ina Nation Land at Buffalo Run
excerpt: A groundbreaking ceremony took place June 23 for a new McDonald's at Buffalo Run on Tsuut'ina Nation land just west of Calgary. It is the first McDonald's in the company's 86-year history to be designed with Indigenous heritage, values, and vision guiding every element of the building. Murals by local Tsuut'ina artists, Tsuut'ina language signage, and architecture shaped by the Tsuut'ina-Taza Cultural Advisory Working Group. Opens late 2026
author: Adam Harrison

title: Global Semiconductor Company Teledyne MEMS Is Investing $20 Million to Expand Its Edmonton Facility
excerpt: Teledyne MEMS is expanding its Edmonton semiconductor manufacturing facility with a $20 million investment, supported by a $620,000 provincial grant. The expansion adds new wafer processing equipment and automation and is expected to create 16 permanent full-time jobs. The Edmonton facility is one of the world's leading MEMS foundries — producing microscopic sensors found in vehicles, medical devices, industrial safety systems, and consumer electronics.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Lethbridge Police Are Warning Residents About a Fake Text Message Claiming You Owe Money for an Unpaid Ticket
excerpt: Lethbridge Police issued a fraud warning on June 22 about a text message scam claiming recipients have unpaid fines or tickets and must click a link to pay. The messages are fraudulent. LPS, the City of Lethbridge, and the Government of Alberta do not send text messages requesting fine payments. Delete the message immediately and do not click anything.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Edmonton Got Nearly 200mm of Rain in June. A New Mosquito That Carries West Nile Virus Is Taking Advantage of It
excerpt: Edmonton's heavy June rainfall has activated dormant mosquito eggs across the city. Several dry years kept the egg population low, so the surge may be smaller than expected. But a new mosquito species called Culex pipiens has been quietly establishing itself in Edmonton since 2018. It is the primary carrier of West Nile virus in North America and it breeds in bird baths, eavestroughs, and rain barrels in your backyard. The city treats public spaces. Your yard is your responsibility.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Lloydminster RCMP Officer Dragged by Stolen Vehicle Before 180 km/h Chase Into Saskatchewan. Driver Now in Custody
excerpt: A Meadow Lake woman is facing five charges after an RCMP officer was dragged by a moving vehicle during an arrest attempt in Lloydminster on June 22. The pursuit reached speeds exceeding 180 km/h and crossed into Saskatchewan before the vehicle turned around and stopped in Lloydminster. The officer was treated in hospital and is recovering at home
author: Adam Harrison

title: Calgary Police Are Looking for a Blue SUV Connected to an Extortion Attempt in Saddle Ridge. The Suspect Sent a Video of the Victim's Own Home
excerpt: Calgary Police are asking the public to help locate a blue 2005-2006 Hyundai Santa Fe with tinted windows, rust on the rear driver's side wheel well, and red tape on the driver's side taillight. The vehicle is connected to an attempted extortion targeting a South Asian resident in the northeast community of Saddle Ridge on May 10. The suspect sent threatening messages and a video of the victim's home while police were actively taking the report.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Just Launched a Council to Fix Its $17 Billion Municipal Infrastructure Crisis
excerpt: Alberta's government announced a Minister's Council on Infrastructure Financing on June 23 to explore new ways for municipalities to fund roads, water systems, transit, and housing infrastructure. The rural infrastructure deficit alone is estimated at $17 billion and growing. The council will report back before the end of 2026. No new funding was announced.
author: Adam Harrison

title: A 25-Year-Old Lethbridge Man Identified as Gunman Who Killed Montreal Police Officer and Innocent Bystander
excerpt: Seth Scott Hatfield, 25, of Lethbridge, Alberta, has been identified by Quebec's coroner as the gunman who opened fire on Montreal police officers outside a Hilton hotel in Côte-des-Neiges on June 22. A Montreal police officer and an innocent bystander were killed. Hatfield died in the exchange. Lethbridge Police conducted a high-risk search at a home on Lemoyne Crescent on Tuesday.
author: Adam Harrison

title: A Woman Is Dead After a Calgary Police Officer Shot Her During a Traffic Stop on Deerfoot Trail. ASIRT Is Investigating
excerpt: Calgary Police shot a woman during a confrontation on Deerfoot Trail between 16 Avenue and Memorial Drive SE at 4:17 p.m. on June 21. She died in hospital Monday morning. Police believed she was the same person who had been driving erratically and fleeing police for 24 hours. Multiple officers used various use-of-force tools before one discharged their firearm. No officers or bystanders were injured. ASIRT is investigating.
author: Shannon Williams

title: Edmonton Is Under an Emergency Water Alert After the Region Got More Rain in One Weekend Than It Normally Gets All Month
excerpt: The City of Edmonton issued an Alberta Emergency Alert at 8:55 p.m. on June 21 ordering all residents to reduce non-essential water use immediately. EPCOR received more than 700 calls for flooding and sewer backups in a single day. The North Saskatchewan River is running at double its normal speed. Nine surrounding communities are under the same alert.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Reverses Beer Price Hike, Deploys Sheriffs to Stampede Tents, and Keeps Fighting Calgary Over Noise
excerpt: Alberta reversed a minimum alcohol price hike days before the Calgary Stampede opens July 3 after Mayor Jeromy Farkas called the province the "fun police." The Sheriffs and Calgary Police signed a first-ever MOU to patrol Stampede tents together. The noise bylaw dispute that threatens Cowboys Music Festival is still unresolved.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Flooding in Stony Plain Alberta Triggers Emergency Alert as Sewer Backups Hit Homes
excerpt: Heavy rain has flooded Stony Plain's wastewater system, and the town says sewer backups are already hitting homes and businesses. An Alberta Emergency Alert issued at 7:14 p.m. on June 21 orders everyone to cut non-essential water use. No laundry, dishes, or showers, and flush only when you have to.
author: Adam Harrison

title: St. Albert Has Issued an Emergency Water Alert. Stop All Non-Essential Water Use Now
excerpt: The City of St. Albert issued an Alberta Emergency Alert at 1:56 p.m. on June 21 ordering all residents to immediately stop all non-essential water use. Heavy rainfall over the past 24 hours has pushed the city's wastewater system to capacity. Localized flooding and sewer backups are already being reported. Beaumont and Leduc County have issued the same alert. Do not shower, flush toilets, run laundry, or use dishwashers until further notice.
author: Adam Harrison

title: New Pickleplex Social Club Is Opening in Airdrie at the Former Peavy Mart Building
excerpt: Pickleplex Social Club is bringing indoor pickleball to Airdrie at 2649 Main Street South in Sierra Springs — the former Peavy Mart building just off Main Street. It will be the company's second Alberta location after Red Deer and its first in the Calgary area. Programming includes beginner sessions, drop-in play, leagues, and tournaments. No opening date confirmed yet.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Energy Rebate 2026: Who Qualifies, How to Apply, and When You Get Paid
excerpt: Alberta is sending $100 to every eligible adult starting July 1. The portal opens Canada Day and closes September 30. You need to be 18 or older, have filed a 2025 tax return, and have household income of $225,000 or less. Married and common-law couples get $200. AISH, ADAP, Alberta Seniors Benefit, and Income Support recipients are enrolled automatically. Payment arrives within two weeks.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Calgary Is the Fastest Growing Startup Ecosystem in Canada, Surging Into Global Top 50
excerpt: The 2026 Global Startup Ecosystem Report ranks Calgary among the world's 41st to 50th fastest emerging tech ecosystems — a jump of 52 positions since 2020. Calgary's ecosystem is growing at four times the Canadian average. Venture capital investment has grown 1,000 percent since 2018. Tech employment grew 78 percent in five years — the fastest in North America.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Edmonton Police Are Looking for a Grey Chrysler Pacifica After Two 14-Year-Old Girls Were Approached and Nearly Lured Into a Vehicle in Southwest Edmonton
excerpt: Two 14-year-old girls were followed and approached by five to six men in a grey Chrysler Pacifica near 18A Avenue SW and Rutherford Road SW on June 14. The men shouted at the girls and drove erratically with the side door open. The girls ran. A bystander called 911. Police need help identifying the vehicle and suspects.
author: Adam Harrison

title: EB Horsman and Son Is Opening a Grande Prairie Branch on August 1. It Is Bringing Product Lines Not Currently Available in the Region
excerpt: EB Horsman and Son, a 126-year-old Western Canadian electrical distributor, is opening its fourth Alberta location in Grande Prairie with a soft opening targeted for August 1, 2026. The full-line branch will stock electrical, lighting, data communications, and Siemens automation products not currently available locally. The company is hiring now for five positions including branch manager, inside sales, and counter sales.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Afternoon Tea Is Back at the Reverend Forbes Homestead in Grande Prairie This Summer. Tickets Are $10 and Booking Is Required
excerpt: The Reverend Forbes Homestead at 10424 96 Street is hosting afternoon teas every Tuesday and Wednesday in July and August 2026 at 2 p.m. Tea, scones, jam, and a guided tour of the homestead are included for $10 per person. Advance booking is required through the Grande Prairie Museum at 780-830-7090.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Grande Prairie Just Rezoned VLA and Montrose to Allow Semi-Detached Homes. What Bylaw C-1228C Means for the Neighbourhood.
excerpt: Grande Prairie City Council gave three readings to Bylaw C-1228C on June 8, 2026, amending the VLA/Montrose Area Redevelopment Plan to allow semi-detached dwellings on properties in the neighbourhood. The change aligns with the neighbourhood plan's long-standing objective of supporting modest housing growth within existing residential areas.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Netflix, Amazon, and Sony Are Filming in Alberta. The Province Just Put $65 Million Behind Its Film Industry to Keep Them Coming.
excerpt: Alberta announced more than $65 million in film and television industry support at the Banff World Media Festival on June 17. The Film and Television Tax Credit is now expanded to cover unscripted TV and game shows for the first time. Since 2020, Alberta productions have generated $1.6 billion in spending and 16,000 jobs. Every government dollar invested generates four dollars back.
author: Adam Harrison

title: A Worker Is Dead After a 300-Ton Crane Crashed Through the Wapiti River Bridge Railing and Plunged Into the River South of Grande Prairie
excerpt: A crane truck crashed through the railing of the Wapiti River Bridge on Highway 40 south of Grande Prairie on June 16 and fell into the Wapiti River below. The worker inside is dead. The crane is still on the river bottom. Divers cannot enter —the current is too strong. RCMP plan to use sonar next week to locate the vehicle.
author: Adam Harrison

title: $25 Million Is Available to Convert Calgary Downtown Office Buildings Into Homes, Hotels, and Student Housing. The Deadline Is July 27
excerpt: Calgary's Downtown Office Conversion Program reopened June 15 with $25 million in city incentive funding available for eligible projects. Hotel conversions now receive $75 per square foot, up from $60. Student housing, senior living, life sciences, and cultural spaces are eligible for the first time. Eight completed projects have already created nearly 800 homes and 226 hotel rooms downtown. Applications close July 27, 2026.
author: Adam Harrison

title: An 11-Year-Old Child Has Died After Going Missing at Echo Dale Regional Park. Medicine Hat's Pools Are Closed Until Further Notice
excerpt: Medicine Hat Police responded to a report of a missing 11-year-old child at Echo Dale Regional Park on Monday June 15 at approximately 2:17 p.m. The child was located at 3:30 p.m. and taken to hospital. The child died. Foul play is not suspected. All city-operated pools and aquatic facilities are closed until further notice. Echo Dale Regional Park remains closed.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Wabamun Lake swimming advisory issued after elevated fecal bacteria found in the water in Parkland County near Edmonton
excerpt: Alberta Health Services has advised against swimming or wading at Camp Yowochas beach on Wabamun Lake in Parkland County after detecting elevated fecal indicator bacteria in the water. Contact can cause gastrointestinal illness and skin, ear and eye infections. The advisory holds until further notice.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Nutrien fined $125,000 after workers sprayed with ammonia on Fort Saskatchewan plant tour
excerpt: Nutrien pleaded guilty to one charge under Alberta's OHS Act after workers touring an ammonia rail car system in Fort Saskatchewan were sprayed with a harmful substance in 2023, seriously injuring one. The company was ordered to pay $125,000 toward ammonia safety training for first responders.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Ministers Head to Asia and Europe This Week to Close Energy and Defence Deals.
excerpt: Energy Minister Brian Jean departs tomorrow for Tokyo and Seoul to pitch Alberta oil to Asian buyers. Jobs Minister Joseph Schow is in Paris today at Eurosatory, the world's largest defence expo, with more than 20 Alberta companies. Both ministers are following up on real deals. Both trips will face scrutiny when the expense reports land.
author: Admin

title: Grande Prairie Fire Advisory in Effect. Your Backyard Fire Pit Is Still Allowed. Your Burn Barrel Probably Is Not
excerpt: A fire advisory took effect for the City of Grande Prairie and the County of Grande Prairie at 3 p.m. on June 5. Conditions improved enough to downgrade from a fire restriction, but the area remains dry. Backyard fire pits with screens are allowed. Open burning, burn barrels, and fireworks without written authorization are not. Wind over 15 km/h means no burning at all.
author: Adam Harrison

title: Grande Prairie's $35 Million Maskwa Medical Centre Is Being Built Next to the Hospital. The City and County Are Being Asked to Back the Loan
excerpt: The Maskwa Medical Centre is a 100,000 square foot non-profit facility under construction next to the Grande Prairie Regional Hospital. Teaching clinic, specialist offices, pharmacy, medical supply store, daycare, restaurant, and parkade. excerpt: The Rec & Ride Youth Pass returns for its second year in Medicine Hat. excerpt: Medicine Hat's Jonn Schoolkate competed at the Alberta Open Bodybuilding Competition in Red Deer on May 30, finishing second in the Men's Classic Physique Novice division.
